english: a Buddhist). — 2. crowd of people, multitude of persons; kŕom-čén (khrom chen) a great crowd; tsógs-pai kŕóm-rnams (tshogs pa'i khrom rnams) the assembled crowd Pth.; p'o-kŕóm (pho khrom) a multitude of men; rgyal-kŕóm (rgyal khrom) prob. an assembly, a gathering of kings Mil.; kŕom dmar-nág k'yil-ba (khrom dmar nag 'khyil ba) a motley crowd, throng of people Pth. Comp. — kŕóm-čén (po) (khrom chen (po)) Thgr. chief market-place, principal street Cs. — kŕóm-dpon (khrom dpon) overseer, police-officer who is charged with the supervision of the market. — kŕóm-skor-ma (khrom skor ma) harlot, strumpet Cs.
Glr., n. of a province (?), kŕóm-po-pa (khrom po pa) an inhabitant of it.
sparkling, glittering, zil-pa kŕom-mé (zil pa khrom me) a sparkling dew-drop Pth.

1. v. 'kŕól-ba ('khrol ba) and 'gról-ba ('grol ba). — 2. a sound; kŕól-gyis soń (khrol gyis song) Glr. (the ring) slid sounding (across the azure-floor); kŕol-dóń (khrol dong) is said to denote a large hand-bell, and kŕol-lóg (khrol log) the same as kŕog-kŕóg (khrog khrog) W. — Cf. 'kŕól-ba ('khrol ba). — 3. kettle (?) v. lčags (lcags).
adj 1. bright, shining, = kŕól-po (khrol po). — 2. kŕol-kŕól byéd-pa (khrol khrol byed pa) Glr., *mig ṭól-lé ṭól-lé tá-wa* (*mig khrol le khrol le lta ba*) C. to stare, la (la), at.
the act of forgiving, pardon Sch.

1. sparkling, glistening, dazzling, e.g. water when the sun shines upon it: *od ṭól-po* (*'od khrol po*) W. brightness, splendour. — 2. distinct, intelligible, *(s)pé-ra ṭól-po zer mi śe(s)* (*(s)pe ra khrol po zer mi she(s)*) W. he cannot speak distinctly.
W. brittle, fragile, opp. to mnyén-po (mnyen po).

an affix to substantives and verbal roots, denoting 1. one who knows a thing thoroughly, making a trade or profession of it, sa-mk'an (sa mkhan) one who knows the country, the road, a guide, a pilot (Dzl. 27, 7); lam-mk'an (lam mkhan) id. Mil.; śiń-mk'an (shing mkhan), worker in wood, carpenter, joiner etc. — 2. affixed to a verbal root, it is often (at least in later literature) equivalent to the periphrastic participle, signifying: he who in any special case performs an action; so 'dógs-mk'an ('dogs mkhan) Glr., one who is binding, fastening; also with an objective case: ṅai bú-mo 'dód-mk'an (nga'i bu mo 'dod mkhan) Glr. such as are courting my daughter; bsád-mk'an (bsad mkhan) the man having killed, the murderer. — 3. In colloquial language, esp. in W., it has on account of its more significant form entirely displaced the proper participle termination in pa (pa): *dúń-ma kyer-k'an-ni mi* (*gdung ma khyer mkhan ni mi*) W. the men carrying the beam; contrary to its original signification, it is even used in a passive sense: *sád-k'an-ni lug* (*bsad mkhan ni lug*) W. the slaughtered sheep.

a clerical teacher, professor, doctor of divinity, principal of a great monastery, abbot, who, as such, is endowed with the mk'an-rgyúd (mkhan rgyud), or spiritual gifts, handed down from Buddha himself by transmission, viz. dbań (dbang), luń (lung), krid (khrid); next to him comes the slób-dpon (slob dpon), or professor in ordinary. mk'án-po tón-mi sámbho-ṭa (mkhan po thon mi sambho Ta) Dr. Thon-mi Sambhoṭa; mk'án-mo (mkhan mo) mistress, instructress Cs.: mk'án-bu (mkhan bu) pupil, scholar Tar.; mk'an-čén (mkhan chen) a great Doctor, a head-master; mk'an-slób (mkhan slob) for mk'án-po (mkhan po) dań (dang) slób-dpon (slob dpon), e.g. blá-ma mk'an-slób-kyi bka (bla ma mkhan slob kyi bka') the words of the Lamas, abbots and masters; also for mk'án-po (mkhan po) dań (dang) slób-ma (slob ma) Glr. 100, 4. — mk'an-rábs (mkhan rabs) the series or succession of the abbots in the great monasteries Cs. — mk'an-rím (mkhan rim) the respective prospects of being elected abbot, as depending on the different ranks of the expectant individuals.

1. heaven, heavens, gen. nam-mk'a (nam mkha'); mk'a-la (mkha' la) in the heavens, mk'á-la p'úr-ba (mkha' la phur ba), rgyú-ba (rgyu ba), ldiń-ba (lding ba) to fly, wander, soar, in the air. — 2. ether, as the fifth element. — 3. symb. num.: cipher, naught. Comp. — mk'a-klóń (mkha' klong), mk'a-k'yáb (mkha' khyab), mk'a-dbyíńs (mkha' dbyings) the whole compass or extent of the
